reason: Classic regulatory capture vector: carves out opt-out pathway from Medicare rate controls for positioned providers while preserving beneficiary nominal coverage—creates two-tier system where affluent patients access premium providers outside rate discipline, shifting adverse selection burden to remaining FFS pool. Erodes Medicare's monopsony pricing power.
vectors: Regulatory capture: exempts specific provider class from rate governance
Market-access exploit: stratifies Medicare into public-rate and private-contract tiers
Adverse selection gaming: enables cherry-picking of low-risk/high-margin cases
Fiscal bleed: reduces effective negotiating leverage, downward pressure on traditional FFS reimbursement
title: A bill to amend title XVIII of the Social Security Act to establish a Medicare payment option for patients and eligible professionals to freely contract, without penalty, for Medicare fee-for-service items and services, while allowing Medicare benificiaries to use their Medicare benefits.
